opinion.h1: HERSHEY MFG. CO. v. ADAMOWSKI, 360 U.S. 717 (1959)
opinion.h4: HERSHEY MFG. CO. v. ADAMOWSKI ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT FOR THE NORTHERN DISTRICT OF ILLINOIS. No. 944. Decided June 29, 1959.
opinion.p: Maurice J. Walsh, John J. Yowell and G. Kent Yowell for appellant.
The judgment is affirmed. Although the District Court had jurisdiction, Doud v. Hodge, 350 U.S. 485, we accept its finding that there was no showing of irreparable injury.
360 U.S. 717 DeGREGORY v. WYMAN, ATTORNEY GENERAL OF NEW HAMPSHIRE. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF NEW HAMPSHIRE. No. 7, Misc. Decided June 29, 1959.
Appeal dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
Louis C. Wyman, Attorney General of New Hampshire, for appellee.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
THE CHIEF JUSTICE, MR. JUSTICE BLACK and MR. JUSTICE DOUGLAS would note probable jurisdiction.
